IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: Please note that this shutdown was previously scheduled for the 20th May 2026 but was cancelled on the day. Some trees have grown too close to overhead power lines in your area. If left unmanaged, branches may come into contact with the lines, which could lead to unexpected interruptions to your electricity supply. To help prevent this, a tree surgeon will cut back the trees. The work has been requested by a third party, and their appointed tree surgeon will carry it out. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so a tree surgeon can safely cut back trees near the overhead power lines. Doing this helps reduce the risk of you experiencing unexpected power cuts in the future and maintains a safe and reliable electricity supply as we appreciate that some customers may have experienced an unplanned power cut in the last 6 months. This work is taking place near Folder Lane. Multi-way traffic lights will be in place to allow the works to be carried out safely.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the overhead cables. This work will cover approximately 300 metres across 2 sections of the network. Due to the scale of the work, the power interruption is approximately 5 hours. Completing this work in one visit will help reduce the risk of future unplanned outages and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place near Boxhill Road.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly inspect, maintain and upgrade the electrical equipment in your area. As part of this programme, we will be upgrading 15 electricity poles. These poles play an important role in supporting the overhead electricity network and helping us deliver a safe and reliable supply to homes and businesses. This work will also reduce the likelihood of unexpected power cuts in the future as we appreciate that some customers may have experienced unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. We understand that power interruptions are inconvenient and we will do everything we can to keep disruption to a minimum. During this time, you will be connected to a generator to provide you with electricity whilst we carry out the works. To connect and disconnect the generator we will need to switch off your power temporarily. This work is taking place near Millers Green Road and Shellow Road.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly inspect, maintain and upgrade the electrical equipment in your area.
As part of this programme, we will be upgrading 5 electricity poles and a piece of equipment called a transformer. We will also be carrying out some tree trimming where necessary. This equipment plays an important role in supporting the overhead electricity network and helping us deliver a safe and reliable supply to homes and businesses. This work will also reduce the likelihood of unexpected power cuts in the future as we appreciate that some customers may have experienced an unplanned power cut in the last 6 months. To carry out this work safely, we need to temporarily switch off your electricity while we work on the electricity poles, as it is not safe to do so with live electricity flowing through them. This work is taking place near Button Street.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hi, it's David from the messaging team at UK Power Networks. I understand how difficult it can be without power and the additional worries this can bring.

For both our business customers and those at home, we will work as quickly and safely as possible to restore supplies. If you need any additional support or have any concerns, please call us on 105.

I will personally continue to keep you updated with our progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
We're sorry your power has gone off again.

We believe there may be an underlying issue causing the recent power cuts, but due to the nature of the electricity network, the heat created by a developing fault can cause a break in a cable to reseal itself. This can make it difficult to locate, so while our engineers are testing our equipment thoroughly to pinpoint the issue, this kind of problem may interrupt power multiple times before it's possible for us to fix.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

I'll keep you updated. Sent by David at 09:55

We know that a power cut can be difficult, and I'm sorry for the length of the time you were without your electricity. Please rest assured that our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Hi, it's David from the messaging team at UK Power Networks. I understand how difficult it can be without power and the additional worries this can bring.

For both our business customers and those at home, we will work as quickly and safely as possible to restore supplies. If you need any additional support or have any concerns, please call us on 105.

I will personally continue to keep you updated with our progress.

We're sorry your power has gone off again.

We believe there may be an underlying issue causing the recent power cuts, but due to the nature of the electricity network, the heat created by a developing fault can cause a break in a cable to reseal itself. This can make it difficult to locate, so while our engineers are testing our equipment thoroughly to pinpoint the issue, this kind of problem may interrupt power multiple times before it's possible for us to fix.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

I'll keep you updated. Sent by David at 09:55

Our staff are working as quickly as they can to get the lights back on, and they've advised us that there are now 7 properties still affected. They have managed to get a further 458 of our customers back on by diverting electricity, so your power may have come on sooner than we originally advised. If you're still off, please don't worry; work on the issue will continue along with SMS updates until everyone is back on. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe.

Steps.Message: We became aware of this power cut at 09:42. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut, but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly. Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.

Logs.Comment:
Sent by David at 10:48 - Our engineers have restored power to all 1518 properties in the HP22 5 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have investigated the report of an overhead cable on the ground and deemed it safe to restore power

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, call us on 08003163105.

Sent by David at 10:24 - Hi David here again. Just to let you know,.  Our engineers are still on site in the HP22 5 area and have advised that they have found an overhead cable laying on the ground which they need to repair and re attach to the pole. At the moment we don't need to change the estimated timeframe.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

For more information, please see your terms of connection, which are usually provided by your supplier but are available here https://UKPN.short.gy/ToC. Thank you.

Sent by David at 13:15 - We believe our engineers have been able to restore power to all 3198 customers in the N13 5, N18 1, N9 9 area by diverting power around a faulted piece of underground network equipment.. We're pleased that we have been able to restore power sooner than we estimated.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We know that being without power can be worrying, but please rest assured that our staff work around the clock to keep the lights on. If you have any concerns or questions, give us a call any time.

Sent by Lisa at 19:00 - Just a quick update to let you know our temporary generator has arrived and engineers are in the process of connecting it. The power may come back on to your area in stages.

We also have tree trimmers working in the area to clear our overhead lines. 

At the moment, we don't need to change the time frame and we'll keep you updated with progress.

Thanks for bearing with us during the power cut today, our staff will work to restore your power as quickly and as safely as possible.

A lot of our customers have questions regarding power cuts, and though you can always reach our team, you can also find some helpful information and answers to some of our most frequently asked questions on our website here - https://UKPN.short.gy/FAQ - Bear in mind this will use your mobile data, so feel free to speak to us if you'd prefer.

We'll be in touch again once we have more information from our staff.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
We're sorry your power has gone off again.

We believe there may be an underlying issue causing the recent power cuts, but due to the nature of the electricity network, the heat created by a developing fault can cause a break in a cable to reseal itself. This can make it difficult to locate, so while our engineers are testing our equipment thoroughly to pinpoint the issue, this kind of problem may interrupt power multiple times before it's possible for us to fix.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

I'll keep you updated. Sent by David at 13:35
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our engineers have now arrived on site in the Regal Close area.

As any issues that currently exist on our network have affected our equipment at the substation, engineers will start their testing there. They will then check our network equipment throughout the area to try and identify any problems, before trying to restore power.

Our estimated time frame of 16:30-17:30  hasn't changed, but if this is not successful it's possible that a cable repair may be needed. As soon as our staff can safely update us, we'll give you the latest news. Sent by David at 13:28.

Steps.Message:
Hi, this is Luke - I wanted to let you know I have just taken over for the night shift, so I'll be here until tomorrow morning making sure you have the latest news from our staff.

I am sorry we will not meet the timeframe given earlier, our engineers need to completely repair and re-erect a section of our overhead network. We will provide a new estimated time as soon as this is viable.

I know that power being off may be especially worrying for you as we head later into the evening, but our field teams work 24 hours so I'll let know further news as soon as it comes in.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
We have just been advised that the power wasn't turned off as an emergency, rather it was the fire in the area that caused damage to our network. Our engineers are hoping to isolate off the main damaged section of cabling which will allow them to restore the majority of supplies.

The remainder will stay off until the emergency services have deemed it safe for our staff to complete any and all repairs needed, including the grounded cable they have located.

We'll continue to keep you updated with any further news we receive. In the mean time though, if you have any questions or concerns, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. Sent by Will at 20:57.
